title: A Worked Sampling Example
concept: Concrete walkthrough: start with noise, predict noise at step 1000, subtract a portion, repeat down to step 0, arriving at a final image. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: DDPM.'

exercises.prompt: If you were sculpting a statue from a rough block of stone, what would you do?

options.text: Slowly chip away bits to reveal the shape
Add stone until a shape appears
Smash the block instantly into a statue

exercises.explain: Yes! You remove tiny pieces bit by bit until the final shape appears — just like our sampling example today!

cards.title: Crossing the Bridge from Noise to Image
cards.body: Imagine standing on one side of a foggy bridge (pure static) and slowly walking across as the fog clears, step by step, until you reach the other side — a clear picture. Today we'll walk that whole bridge, one step at a time, with real numbers!

cards.title: What Is a Worked Sampling Example?
cards.body: It's a step-by-step walkthrough showing exactly how an AI turns pure random noise into a final image. Starting at step 1000 (all noise), the model predicts the noise, subtracts a little of it, and moves to step 999. This repeats all the way down to step 0, the finished image.

cards.title: Melting an Ice Sculpture in Reverse
cards.body: Think of it like watching a block of ice slowly turn into a detailed sculpture, but backwards: instead of melting, tiny bits of 'ice' (noise) are carved away in the correct spots each round, until the hidden shape (image) is revealed at the very end.

cards.title: Walking Down from Step 1000
cards.body:
Step 1000: Total static, no shape at all.
Step 750: The model predicts noise, removes some — a few blurry blobs appear.
Step 500: More noise removed — rough shapes of a face show up.
Step 250: Details sharpen — eyes and a smile appear.
Step 0: Final clear image — a smiling face, fully formed!

cards.title: Myth: The Image Appears All at Once
cards.body: Myth: The AI jumps straight from noise to a perfect picture in one move. Truth: It takes many small steps — often hundreds or thousands — each one removing just a little noise, not the whole thing at once.

cards.title: One Big Jump vs. Many Small Steps
cards.body: Removing noise all at once creates a messy, broken image. Removing it gradually, step by step, creates a clean, realistic final picture.

left.text: Noise removed in a single leap → blurry, distorted result

right.text: Noise removed gradually, step 1000 → step 0 → sharp, clear image

exercises.prompt: In our worked example, what happens at step 1000?

options.text: It's pure random noise, no shape yet
Half the noise has been removed

exercises.explain: Correct! Step 1000 is the very start — just random static with no clear shape yet.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The model removes all the noise in one single step.
exercises.explain: False — the model removes just a small portion of noise at each step, repeating many times until reaching step 0.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea.
exercises.sentence: At each step, the model predicts the noise, subtracts a portion of it, and moves one step closer to ___.
exercises.explain: Each step nudges the picture closer to the clean, final image — that's the whole point of the process!

exercises.prompt: Which best describes going from step 500 to step 0 in our worked example?

options.text: Rough shapes slowly become sharp, clear details
More noise is added back in

exercises.explain: Right! As we move from step 500 down to step 0, the model keeps removing small bits of noise, so shapes get sharper and more detailed.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: Summarize the worked sampling example from step 1000 to step 0.

options.text: Start with pure noise, predict and subtract a little noise each step, repeat until reaching a clear final image at step 0
Start with a clear image and add noise until step 1000
Jump directly from step 1000 to step 0 in one move
Randomly guess the final image without any steps

exercises.explain: Exactly right! Starting from total noise at step 1000, the model predicts and removes a little noise at a time, walking step by step down to a clean final image at step 0. Next up: DDPM.
